rationale: The cited 2000 paper appears in an early grouped citation as prior evidence that postpromotion dips can be detected in weekly/store-level sales data. In the main development of the 2007 paper, the authors build their contribution around household-level choice-incidence-quantity modeling and simulation decomposition, and they do not assign a distinct methodological or conceptual role to the 2000 study. Because the citation functions as general background context rather than a specific building block for hypotheses, model structure, or identification, this is a passing mention.
